commit.message:
feat: replace WalHeadMap with O(1) WalDirectIndex for WAL lookups (#1302)

Replace the binary-search-based WalHeadMap (16-shard sorted arrays, O(log n))
with WalDirectIndex — per-table contiguous ulong[] arrays indexed by recordId
for O(1) cache-friendly reads.

Architecture:
- ConcurrentDictionary<uint, TableSlot> for table lookup (~10-50 tables)
- Per-table TableSlot: contiguous ulong[] indexed directly by recordId
- Lock-free reads via Volatile.Read on array reference and elements
- Growth via lock + copy (power-of-2, capped at +1M per growth)
- Tombstoning: value 0 = deleted/empty (NullPtr)
- LiveCount tracked via Interlocked for snapshot enumeration

Same API surface (TryGetHead, SetHead, BatchSetHeads, BulkLoad, CopyArrays,
Count, Dispose) — drop-in replacement for WalHeadMap.

Fixes #1302

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Align Rust library name with directory name

to match the package name and directory structure. This produces
librender_sys.a instead of librender.a, eliminating naming confusion.

- Makefile: RUST_LIB path
- README.md: Manual build instructions and go generate examples

- internal/render/render_test.go: Documentation comment

- scripts/README.md: Output artifacts section

Also fixed pre-existing Rust compilation error:
- render-sys/src/drm.rs: Cast c_ulong to c_int for ioctl call
  (required for nix::libc::ioctl signature compatibility)

Validation:
- Build succeeds: make clean && make build ✓
- Correct output: librender_sys.a created ✓
- Static linking confirmed: ldd bin/wain reports 'not a dynamic executable' ✓
- Tests pass: make test-go (all pass except hardware-dependent GPU tests) ✓

Resolves AUDIT.md LOW severity finding: Inconsistent naming for render-sys Rust crate

commit.message:
feat: add resilience - outbox pattern, bounded channels, dead-letter handler

Outbox Pattern:
- OutboxMessage entity persisted in same DB transaction as domain ops
- DomainEventInterceptor now writes to outbox (SavingChanges) instead of publishing directly
- OutboxProcessor polls every 1s, deserializes and publishes to channel (batch 50, max 5 retries)
- OutboxCleaner removes processed messages older than 3 days (hourly)
- OutboxDbContext + OutboxMessage mapped in all 5 module DbContexts via extension method

Bounded Channels:
- Pre-created bounded channels (capacity 500) with backpressure for all event types

Dead-Letter Handler:
- DeadLetterEventBusErrorHandler replaces DefaultEventBusErrorHandler
- Logs CRITICAL when events exhaust all retries for audit trail

Reflection Cache:
- Type name cache in DomainEventInterceptor (ConcurrentDictionary)
- MethodInfo cache in OutboxProcessor for TryWriteAsync generic dispatch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: info panel opens under sidebar instead of into content area (#312)

## Problem
Clicking the ℹ︎ button next to the session name in the expanded view
caused the info panel to partially open under the left sidebar pane.

## Root Cause
The `.info-panel` in `ExpandedSessionView.razor.css` used `right:
-0.5rem` positioning, which made the dropdown extend **leftward** from
the trigger. When the trigger was near the left edge of the content area
(right after the sidebar), the panel extended past the content boundary
and was clipped by `overflow: hidden` on `.expanded-card`, making it
appear to open under the sidebar.

## Fix
Changed `right: -0.5rem` → `left: 0` so the panel extends **rightward**
into the content area. This matches the existing pattern used by the
sidebar header's info panel in `SessionSidebar.razor.css`.

## Testing
- 5 new regression tests in `InfoPanelPositionTests.cs` verify the panel
uses left-anchored positioning
- All 2110 tests pass
- Mac Catalyst build succeeds

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Bump version to 0.16.16 and update changelog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: Include featured images without _wp_attached_file metadata in scoped migrations

Critical fix for incomplete media migrations when selected post types are specified.

Problem: When migrating specific post types, featured images were being filtered out
if they lacked '_wp_attached_file' metadata. This happened because:
1. get_media_ids_for_selected_post_types() correctly collected featured images
2. But get_media_files() applied _wp_attached_file filter to ALL media
3. Featured images from draft/private posts often lack this metadata
4. Result: Collected IDs were filtered out = featured images not migrated

Solution: Only apply _wp_attached_file filter when migration is unscoped (all media).
When scoped to specific post types, trust the explicit ID collection which includes
featured images and content-referenced media regardless of metadata.

This fixes the issue where users reported 'not all media migrated' - especially
featured images that were set through WordPress admin but not through Bricks/Gutenberg.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: guard chat participant registration to prevent activation failure

The activate() function was throwing when vscode.chat.createChatParticipant
was called (if Copilot Chat extension unavailable), which prevented all
subsequent command registrations including aether.dashboard.

Fix: wrap registerChatParticipant in try-catch in extension.ts, and add
null guard for vscode.chat API in participant.ts. Dashboard commands now
always register regardless of chat availability.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add project-level uv.toml to use PyPI for this project

Project-level uv.toml overrides global ~/.config/uv/uv.toml settings.
This ensures the project always uses public PyPI, even on machines with
corporate Artifactory configured globally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: dim_by_page key lookup uses 'page' not 'page_number' for polygon normalization

PageDimensions.to_dict() serializes as {"page": N}, not {"page_number": N}.
The old lookup created an empty dim_by_page dict, causing polygon coordinates
to be stored as raw inches instead of normalized [0,1] values.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: set socket backlog to 8192 for burst traffic

Increases the TCP listen backlog from the OS default (~128) to 8192,
allowing Kestrel to queue more pending connections during traffic
bursts without dropping SYNs.

Closes #1304

commit.message:
Fix duplicate cleanup action scheduling in Action Scheduler

- Added unschedule of existing efs_cleanup_migration_log before scheduling new one
- Prevents accumulation of duplicate cleanup actions when migration completes
- Root cause: Completion block was reached multiple times, scheduling new cleanup
  without removing existing ones - Action Scheduler queue filled up with duplicates

This fixes the issue where many efs_cleanup_migration_log jobs accumulated
in the queue (6+ pending), all scheduled for 7 days in future.

Implement minimal Wayland event processing in App.Run()

Adds minimal implementation of processWaylandEvents() that:
- Flushes pending outbound requests to prevent deadlock
- Returns immediately (no inbound event reading yet)
- Documents what's still needed with detailed TODO comment

This addresses the HIGH severity audit finding about incomplete
Wayland event dispatch while being safe to call in the event loop.

Full event reading/dispatching remains a future enhancement requiring:
1. Wire protocol event parsing from socket
2. Event handler registration and dispatch to objects
3. Frame callback handling for render synchronization

Tests: All pass (make test-go)
Complexity: Increased 2→3 (+50%) due to error handling branch

Full BQL pipeline operational:
  'tasks | where not done and due < today | sort due | limit 10'
  → tokenise → parse AST → compile SQL → execute → typed rows

394 total workspace tests pass.

commit.message:
Fix migration hang at 83% during re-migrations

Critical fixes for headless migration stalling:

1. Mark CSS phase as 100% complete (async-migration-runner.php)
   - Added missing update_progress() call after global CSS migration
   - Allows phase to transition properly instead of hanging at 75%

2. Clear processed_post_ids from old migrations (async-migration-runner.php)
   - Explicitly set processed_post_ids and processed_media_ids to empty arrays
   - Prevents re-migrations from inheriting 'already processed' IDs from prior runs
   - Fixes 0/480 display and allows templates to be re-processed

3. Safety guard for stale processed IDs (batch-phase-runner.php)
   - If processed_count=0 (new run), clear any stale processed_ids from checkpoint
   - Prevents filtering out all remaining IDs due to idempotency check

Root cause: Re-migrations loaded checkpoints containing processed_ids from
previous migrations. New batch run would have processed_count=0 but
processed_ids_set={480 IDs from prior run}, so ALL remaining IDs were filtered
out as 'already processed'. Batch loop had nothing to do, phase never completed.

This prevents the infinite re-enqueue cycle where Action Scheduler would keep
retrying the same stuck state without making progress.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: make /dashboard/health auth-optional and fix Cosmos attribute name

- Skip auth + group isolation middleware for /dashboard/health so it
  can be used as a diagnostic probe without a token
- Use auth user if available, fall back to 'diagnostic-probe' placeholder
- Fix _container → _usage_container in both dashboard.py and health.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use x86-64-v3 preset for IlcInstructionSet

.NET 10 ILC bundles fma, bmi2, lzcnt into the avx2/x86-64-v3
preset. Individual names (fma, bmi2, lzcnt) are not recognised
and cause AOT compile failure.

feat(tools): 13 tools complete — Sprint A + Sprint B (#47)

* feat(tools): Sprint B Wave 3 — test scenes, VFX bench, GDD checker, collision matrix, frame data, watcher

Closes #38

Closes #40

Closes #41

Closes #42

Closes #43

Closes #45

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(tools): Sprint A Wave 2 -- scene checker, project validator, integration gate

Closes #33
Closes #35
Closes #37

Built 3 critical automation tools for M3 integration discipline:

Tool 5: Scene Integrity Checker (check-scenes.py)
- Scans all .tscn files in games/ashfall/scenes/
- Validates script references exist on disk
- Checks collision_layer/mask against expected scheme
- Reports PASS/FAIL with line numbers

Tool 6: Godot Headless Validator (validate-project.py)
- Validates project.godot structure
- Checks [autoload] files exist
- Validates [input] actions are well-formed
- Verifies main_scene exists
- Runs autoload checker and signal validator as sub-checks
- Comprehensive project health report

Tool 7: Integration Gate Automation (integration-gate.py + workflow)
- Runs ALL validators in sequence
- Reports overall PASS/WARN/FAIL
- GitHub Action on push to main
- Auto-creates issues on failure with details
- This is the ceremony that prevents dead code syndrome

All tools tested and working. Integration gate passed all checks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Joaquin Perez del Real <joperezd@microsoft.com>

commit.message:
Add BQL SQL compiler

New query/compile.rs: AST → SQL with parameterised bind values.

Features:
- Field validation per source (unknown field → compile error)
- Type checking (has only on tags, ranges only on dates)
- Operators: =, !=, <, >, <=, >= → direct SQL mapping
- has #tag → subquery: IN (SELECT page_id FROM tags WHERE tag = ?)
- Ranges (this week, last month, etc.) → date range with chrono
- none → IS NULL / IS NOT NULL
- backlinks.count → correlated subquery
- $page, $today → parameterised bind values
- count → wraps in SELECT COUNT(*)
- group + count → per-group counts via GROUP BY

22 compiler tests + 33 parser tests = 55 BQL tests.
382 total workspace tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
